commands.repair_reason: Contributor and maintainer action is needed to fix the audit-log race and supply real after-fix proof; automation should not take the repair lane because the proof must come from a real setup.; Needs attention: the patch improves historical credential scrubbing but rewrites a live append-only audit log via rename without detecting concurrent appends.; Review finding: [P2] Avoid overwriting concurrent audit appends —... (sha=1139a1ea2ad2d8aafc799c269f18a52a00d5a1fd)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a config-audit scrub helper, doctor health contribution, and tests to redact historical `argv`/`execArgv` values in `config-audit.jsonl` via `openclaw doctor --fix`.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main redacts only new process snapshots and appends audit records without any historical rewrite path. I did not execute the CLI in this read-only review, but https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80777 includes real before-state grep evidence from an affected log.
commands.review_followup: Contributor and maintainer action is needed to fix the audit-log race and supply real after-fix proof; automation should not take the repair lane because the proof must come from a real setup.

target.title: fix(config-audit): scrub pre-redactor argv values from historical config-audit.jsonl entries
target.author: yb0y
